purpose.summary: A single, unified-brand e-commerce and portfolio site for Kelly Laura, a landscape painter and vintage clothing curator. The site sells original paintings and one-of-a-kind vintage clothing directly, accepts commission inquiries, and presents her work with the visual seriousness of a small gallery website.

success_criteria.criterion: Kelly can independently publish a new painting or vintage item via Sanity Studio in under 5 minutes, without developer involvement.
A customer can complete a purchase (original painting OR vintage item) end-to-end on mobile in under 3 minutes, including shipping address entry.
Each product is unique inventory (qty=1); the site never sells the same physical object twice.
Commission inquiries arrive in Kelly's inbox as structured email within 60 seconds of submission.
Lighthouse mobile performance score >= 90 on home, gallery, and product detail pages.
All product imagery is served via next/image with appropriate sizes; no raw img tags in the codebase.

explicitly_out_of_scope.item: Prints, reproductions, or product variants (each item is one-of-a-kind)
Multi-currency or international tax calculation beyond Stripe defaults
Blog / journal / studio diary (deferred to v1.1)
Newsletter signup wired to ESP (capture email to Sanity in v1, integrate later)
Search (browse is sufficient at launch inventory volume)
Returns automation (manual process, contact form sufficient)

framework.name: Next.js
framework.rendering: Server Components by default; Client Components only where interactivity requires

fonts.display: Cormorant Garamond (weights 300–700, regular + italic), via next/font/google. Its true italic carries the cursive editorial accent.
fonts.body: Cormorant Garamond for paragraph copy. Inter Tight is retained behind --font-ui for the small caps-tracked labels (.text-ui) where Cormorant becomes spidery.

cms.name: Sanity
cms.studio_location: /studio route, mounted in the same Next.js app

commerce.payments: Stripe Checkout (hosted, not Elements) for v1
commerce.reasoning: Hosted Checkout removes PCI scope, gives us Apple Pay / Google Pay / Link with no extra work, and is the right default for a low-volume, high-AOV store. Migrate to Elements later only if checkout customization becomes a real constraint.
commerce.inventory_model: Each product is qty=1, one-of-a-kind. Inventory state lives in Sanity (status field: available | reserved | sold). Stripe is payment only, not inventory.

observability.logging: Vercel runtime logs; structured console.log with request IDs

forbidden.item: Shopify, BigCommerce, Snipcart, any commerce platform other than Stripe
Inter, Roboto, or system fonts as the primary BODY face. Inter Tight is reserved for caps-tracked UI labels (.text-ui) only; do not use it for paragraph copy.
Material UI, Chakra, Ant Design, Bootstrap
Redux, Zustand, MobX (React Server Components + URL state + form actions are sufficient)
localStorage / sessionStorage for cart state (use cookies via server actions)

route.name: Home
route.purpose: Brand introduction, hero painting, two-section split into Paintings and Vintage, featured works strip, about teaser, newsletter capture.
route.data: Sanity: featured paintings (4), hero work (1), about-strip excerpt

route.name: Paintings Index
route.purpose: Browse all available paintings. Filterable by status (available / sold) and series/tag.
route.data: Sanity: all paintings, sorted by createdAt desc by default
route.ui: Asymmetric editorial grid; varied aspect ratios respected; sold pieces are visible but visually distinct (subtle overlay + "Sold" label).

route.name: Painting Detail
route.purpose: Single painting product page. Primary purchase surface for originals.
route.data: Sanity: full painting record; related works (3, by tag overlap)

elements.element: Full-bleed primary image with secondary detail images in a vertical strip
Buy button -> Stripe Checkout (sold-out state if unavailable)

route.name: Vintage Index
route.purpose: Browse vintage clothing. Filterable by category (dresses, outerwear, knitwear, etc.), size, and era.
route.data: Sanity: all vintage items, available first

elements.element: Multiple photos including condition close-ups and measurement reference
Era, size (with flat measurements, not just label size), material, condition notes

route.name: Commissions
route.purpose: Explain Kelly's commission process; submit an inquiry.

elements.element: Typical timeline and price ranges (not a price list)
Inquiry form: name, email, project type, budget range, timeline, message, optional reference image upload

route.name: About / Studio
route.purpose: Kelly's story, practice, location, influences. Editorial long-form.

route.name: Checkout Success
route.purpose: Post-purchase confirmation; triggered by Stripe redirect.
route.behavior: Verifies session via Stripe API; updates Sanity item status to "sold"; sends email via Resend.

route.name: Checkout Cancel
route.purpose: Graceful return after abandoned checkout. Restores reservation state if applicable.

route.name: Checkout Session API
route.purpose: Creates Stripe Checkout session; reserves inventory in Sanity.

route.name: Stripe Webhook
route.purpose: Authoritative inventory state update. Handles checkout.session.completed and checkout.session.expired.

route.name: Commission Form Handler
route.purpose: Validates inquiry; sends email via Resend; stores submission in Sanity.

route.name: Sanity Studio
route.purpose: Mounted Sanity Studio for Kelly's content editing.
route.auth: Sanity's own auth; no app-level auth required

field.name: medium
field.description: e.g., Oil on linen, Soft pastel on sanded paper

field.name: reservedUntil
field.description: Set when checkout session opens; cleared on completion or expiry

field.name: labelSize
field.description: As marked on garment, may be empty

schema.name: commissionExample
schema.description: Past commission for the public gallery

inventory_and_checkout_flow.description: Critical: every product is one-of-a-kind. The race condition between two customers initiating checkout simultaneously is the single most important correctness concern in this system. Spec follows.

step.name: Initiate Checkout
step.trigger: Customer clicks Buy on a product detail page.
step.action: POST /api/checkout with productType + productId. Server reads current product status from Sanity. If status != "available", return 409. If available, atomically set status="reserved", reservedUntil=now+30min, stripeSessionId=null (pending), and only then create the Stripe Checkout session with metadata { productType, productId, sanityRevisionId }. Persist sessionId back to Sanity. Return Stripe Checkout URL.
step.atomicity_note: Use Sanity's optimistic concurrency via the patch().ifRevisionId() API. If the patch fails because another request beat us, return 409 to the customer with a "just sold" message.

step.name: Webhook Confirms Sale
step.action: Verify webhook signature. Look up product by sessionId in Sanity. Set status="sold", soldAt=now, clear reservedUntil and stripeSessionId. Trigger Resend emails (customer confirmation, Kelly notification).
step.idempotency: Webhook handler must be idempotent. Stripe retries on non-2xx. If product is already "sold" with matching sessionId, return 200 without changes.

step.name: Expiry / Cancel
step.trigger: Stripe sends checkout.session.expired, OR scheduled cron runs.
step.action: Find products where reservedUntil < now AND status = "reserved". Reset status to "available", clear reservation fields.
step.cron: Vercel Cron at /api/cron/release-stale-reservations every 10 minutes. Defense in depth; webhook is primary.

step.name: Success Page UX
step.action: /checkout/success verifies session via Stripe API server-side. Does NOT update inventory (webhook owns that). Renders confirmation with order summary. Safe to refresh.

design_system.surface_mode: Dark editorial. Deep charcoal surface, warm cream type, ochre accent. Decided 2026-05-31 — explicit user pivot away from the original bone/light surface to a Galleria-style dark gallery. Token names are preserved from the original light palette so existing utilities still compile; what changed is the role each colour plays: • ink / ink-soft → SURFACES (page background, elevated cards) • bone / bone-deep → TEXT (primary, muted) • ochre / ochre-deep → warm accent (kept warm, brightened slightly for contrast on dark) • rule → subtle hairlines on dark

color.name: ochre
color.role: background accent only — used for the 18+ tile badge and inside placeholder image gradients. NEVER as a text colour.

color.name: ochre-deep
color.role: background accent only. NEVER as a text colour, focus border, or link hover.

colour_rules.rule: Ochre never appears in text. No `text-ochre`, `text-ochre-deep`, `hover:text-ochre*`, `group-hover:text-ochre*`, or `decoration-ochre` in the codebase. Italic Cormorant is the accent — colour is not.
Button hovers do not flash ochre either. Cream CTA buttons (bg-bone text-ink) shift to bg-bone-deep on hover, not to ochre.

Form focus borders use border-bone, not border-ochre. The ochre tokens survive purely as decoration: the small 18+ badge background and the tonal warmth inside the placeholder image gradients (ProductImage).

step.name: meta
step.usage: .text-meta — small italic body for caption / metadata strings (medium, size, year, count). Replaces the old uppercase-mono atmosphere classes everywhere.

step.name: roman
step.usage: .text-roman — small-caps Cormorant for Roman numerals (I, II, III…) and short section indices. Non-italic, slightly letter-spaced.

step.name: ui
step.usage: .text-ui — reserved for actual buttons / form submit / disclosure triggers only. NOT for section labels or list-group headers — those use .text-meta or .text-roman instead.

typography.pairing_rule:
Italic Cormorant Garamond is the dominant voice, not a sparing accent. Page headings, product titles, section labels, captions, field labels, nav links, and inline accents inside headings all run italic Cormorant. Inter Tight (.text-ui) is the only sans on the site and is used only on actual buttons and tappable form controls — never on body copy, labels, or section markers. If you find yourself reaching for `text-ui` on a `
` or a ``, use `.text-meta` instead.
typography.case_voice: Lowercase italic Cormorant is the gallery voice — "choose a doorway", "vintage, found slowly", "letters from the studio". Roman numerals (.text-roman) carry short capitalised indices when something needs a marker. The old all-caps display style (`font-display-caps`) has been removed from page headings; case-as-volume is no longer in the rotation.

spatial.philosophy: Dense card grids, uniform tiles. Product / category grids run 2-col on mobile, 3-col tablet, up to 5-col on wide desktop — modelled on documentation/samples/Screenshot.png. The old editorial vertical offsets on PaintingCard / VintageCard were removed so the grid reads as a coherent index, not a staircase. Generous negative space still surrounds hero imagery, and body copy is never centre-aligned (but short editorial headlines may be — e.g., the home hero).
spatial.card_anatomy: Product / doorway tiles all share the same anatomy: cover image (uniform 4:5 aspect) above a caption block. The caption block is Roman numeral (.text-roman) + count (.text-meta) on the same baseline row, followed by an italic Cormorant title below. No overlay captions; labels sit BELOW the image. See components/site/ CategoryDoorways.tsx for the canonical implementation.

motion.principle: Restraint. One well-orchestrated entrance per section beats five micro-interactions.
motion.hero_entrance: Staggered fade-up on load, 150ms increments, cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.6, 0.2, 1)
motion.hover: Image scale 1.03 on 600ms ease for product cards; underline-grow on nav links
motion.forbidden: Bouncy springs, page-load spinners on Server Component routes, parallax scrolling, scroll-jacking

design_system.reference: Visual direction is set by documentation/samples/Screenshot.png — a dark editorial gallery layout with small museum-plaque tiles on a near-black surface, Roman-numeral indices, and elegant serif headlines. Treat it as the source of truth for surface tone, tile rhythm, and caption style. Where earlier mockups used a bone/light surface, those are superseded.

integration.name: Sanity
integration.client: @sanity/client; use next-sanity helpers; cache fetches with Next.js cache tags
integration.revalidation: On-demand revalidation via Sanity webhook -> /api/revalidate. Tag-based: paintings, vintage, about, settings.

integration.name: Stripe
integration.products: Created dynamically per session; do not pre-create Stripe products for one-off inventory
integration.shipping: Use Stripe's shipping address collection; flat-rate options pulled from Sanity siteSettings
integration.tax: Stripe Tax enabled; Kelly registers in Indiana initially

integration.name: Vercel
integration.env: VERCEL_URL handled automatically; set SITE_URL for production

phase.name: Foundation
phase.goal: Project scaffolded, deployed, fonts and tokens loading, empty pages routable.

tasks.task: create-next-app with TypeScript, Tailwind v4, App Router
Configure ESLint + Prettier + strict TS settings
Set up Vercel project, connect repo, configure preview deploys
Add design tokens to globals.css via @theme directive (Tailwind v4)
Wire next/font for Cormorant Garamond (display + body) and Inter Tight (UI labels only)
Scaffold all routes from information_architecture as empty pages
Build shared Nav and Footer components matching the mockup

phase.exit_criteria: Production deploy at temporary Vercel URL loads with correct typography and palette on every route, even if pages are stubs.

phase.name: Sanity + Schemas
phase.goal: Sanity project live, all schemas defined, Studio mounted, sample content seeded.

tasks.task: Create Sanity project; configure CORS for local + production origins
Implement all schemas from data_model section
Mount Studio at /studio-admin route
Write GROQ queries for: paintings list, painting by slug, vintage list, vintage by slug, featured paintings, site settings, about page
Set up next-sanity client with cache tags and revalidation webhook
Seed 3 paintings and 3 vintage items for development

phase.exit_criteria: Kelly can log into Studio, create a painting with images, and see it appear at /paintings/[slug] on the dev deploy within 30 seconds.

phase.name: Storefront Pages
phase.goal: All public-facing pages built and matching mockup quality.

tasks.task: Home page: hero, marquee, two-section split, featured works grid, about strip, newsletter capture
Paintings index with filter UI (status, series, tag) via URL search params
Painting detail page with image gallery, metadata, Buy button (non-functional placeholder)
Vintage index and detail pages
Studio/About page from singleton
Legal pages (rendered from siteSettings block content)
Mobile responsive pass on every page
Metadata, OpenGraph, structured data (Product schema) per page

phase.exit_criteria: Site is fully browsable. Buy buttons present but route to a "checkout coming soon" placeholder. Lighthouse mobile >= 90 on home, paintings index, painting detail.

phase.name: Commerce
phase.goal: End-to-end checkout works, including the inventory race condition handling.

tasks.task: Implement /api/checkout with Sanity optimistic concurrency reservation
Stripe Checkout session creation with product metadata
Webhook handler at /api/webhooks/stripe with signature verification and idempotency
Vercel Cron job for stale reservation cleanup
Resend integration with React Email templates
/checkout/success page with session verification
/checkout/cancel page
Sold-state UI on product cards and detail pages
Test the race: open two browsers, click Buy simultaneously, verify exactly one wins

phase.exit_criteria: A test purchase using a Stripe test card completes: Sanity status flips to sold, both emails arrive, success page shows order summary. Race condition test passes.

phase.name: Commissions

tasks.task: /commissions page with process, examples gallery, inquiry form
Form with client-side validation, optional image upload to Sanity
/api/commission-inquiry handler: validate, store in Sanity, email Kelly via Resend
Spam protection: honeypot field + rate limiting by IP

phase.exit_criteria: A submitted inquiry creates a Sanity record and arrives in Kelly's inbox within 60 seconds.

phase.name: Pre-Launch Hardening

tasks.task: Sentry integration for client and server errors
Custom 404 and 500 pages matching brand
robots.txt, sitemap.xml (dynamic from Sanity)
Cookie banner if analytics requires (Vercel Analytics + Plausible may not)
Accessibility audit: keyboard nav, focus states, alt text on every image, color contrast on all text
Performance audit: bundle analysis, image sizes, font loading strategy
Domain setup, SSL, DNS
Stripe account moved from test to live mode
Final content review with Kelly: copy on every page, legal text, product listings

phase.exit_criteria: kellylauraart.com loads in production with live Stripe keys, real inventory, and Kelly can complete a real-money test transaction.

code.rule: Server Components are the default. Add "use client" only when state, effects, or browser APIs are required.
Data fetching lives in /lib/sanity/queries.ts. No fetch() calls in components.
All Sanity queries are typed via sanity-codegen or hand-written types in /types/sanity.ts.
Route handlers return NextResponse with explicit status codes. Never throw to the framework.
Environment variables accessed only through /lib/env.ts, validated at boot with zod.
No client-side state libraries. URL search params for filters, server actions for forms, cookies for cart-equivalent state.

git.rule: Conventional commits (feat:, fix:, chore:, refactor:, docs:)
Trunk-based; feature branches short-lived; PRs preferred for any change touching commerce or webhooks
Never commit secrets; .env.local gitignored; production secrets only in Vercel dashboard

testing.philosophy: Test the parts that hurt when they break. Inventory race conditions and webhook handlers must have tests. Pixel-perfect UI tests are out of scope.

required_tests.test: Inventory reservation: concurrent requests, only one succeeds
Stripe webhook idempotency: duplicate delivery does not double-update
Stale reservation cleanup: reservation older than 30min is released

testing.runner: Vitest for unit/integration; Playwright optional for one end-to-end checkout flow

risk.name: Concurrent checkout race
risk.description: Two customers click Buy at the same moment. Without proper concurrency control, both proceed to Stripe and one will pay for a product the other has already bought.
risk.mitigation: Optimistic concurrency in Sanity via ifRevisionId; webhook as final authority; 30-minute reservation TTL.

risk.name: Webhook delivery failures
risk.description: If Stripe webhook fails to deliver, Sanity inventory stays "reserved" while the customer has paid.
risk.mitigation: Idempotent handler; Stripe retries automatically; defense-in-depth cron job; Sentry alert on webhook errors.

risk.name: Image size on art-heavy pages
risk.description: Paintings deserve large, high-quality images, but bandwidth and Core Web Vitals push the other way.
risk.mitigation: next/image with appropriate sizes; AVIF/WebP via Sanity image pipeline; LCP image preloaded on detail pages.

risk.name: Shipping cost variability
risk.description: Flat-rate shipping in v1 will under- or over-charge for some pieces. Large paintings cost much more to ship than small ones.
risk.mitigation: Per-piece shippingNotes field; Kelly can refund difference manually for v1; revisit calculated shipping in v1.1.

risk.name: Content lock-in to Sanity
risk.description: If Sanity changes pricing or terms, migration is non-trivial.
risk.mitigation: Accepted. Sanity is mature, has a stable API, and Portable Text is widely supported. Migration path exists if ever needed.

deliverables_summary.item: Working purchase flow for paintings and vintage items
README with local setup, env variable list, deployment notes
RUNBOOK with: how to add a product, how to handle a failed payment, how to release a stuck reservation, how to refund
